title: Reuse a key and it vanishes: cipher1 XOR cipher2 = plain1 XOR plain2
setup:
# --- plot helper (hidden) — the two-time pad leak ---
# The leak curve is the student's OWN two_time_leak of two ciphertexts made with
# their otp_encrypt. Falls back to a built-in XOR until those work. The
# plain1-XOR-plain2 curve is the fixed proof the key cancelled out.
def __plot():
    p1 = "attack base"
    p2 = "defend zone"
    key = [42, 200, 13, 99, 7, 250, 1, 128, 64, 33, 170]
    direct = [ord(a) ^ ord(b) for a, b in zip(p1, p2)]
    try:
        c1 = otp_encrypt(p1, key)   # <- the STUDENT's function
        c2 = otp_encrypt(p2, key)   # <- the STUDENT's function
        leak = two_time_leak(c1, c2)   # <- the STUDENT's function
        if isinstance(leak, list) and len(leak) == len(direct):
            leak_pts = [[i, leak[i]] for i in range(len(leak))]
            llabel = "cipher1 XOR cipher2 (your leak)"
        else:
            raise ValueError()
    except Exception:
        c1 = [ord(c) ^ k for c, k in zip(p1, key)]
        c2 = [ord(c) ^ k for c, k in zip(p2, key)]
        leak = [a ^ b for a, b in zip(c1, c2)]
        leak_pts = [[i, leak[i]] for i in range(len(leak))]
        llabel = "cipher1 XOR cipher2 (the leak)"
    return [
        {"name": llabel, "points": leak_pts, "highlight": True},
        {"name": "plain1 XOR plain2 (key gone!)", "points": [[i, direct[i]] for i in range(len(direct))], "highlight": False},
    ]

caption.todo: Finish otp_encrypt and two_time_leak to expose the reused-key leak.
caption.tuning: Your leak is showing — keep tuning until it matches the target.
caption.match: ✅ Your two-time-pad leak matches the exact answer!
